title: Solutions of inequalities: graphical
description: Given the graph of a two-variable linear inequality, check whether a given pair of values is a solution of the inequality.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Is $(-2,2)$ a solution of the graphed inequality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$(-2,2)$ is within the shaded area.



[[☃ image 1]]
Yes, $(-2,2)$ is a solution of the graphed inequality.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes both scale by one. There is a dashed line representing an inequality that goes through the points negative four, three and four, negative three. The shaded region is above the line.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Is $(1,3)$ a solution of the graphed inequality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$(1,3)$ is within the shaded area.



[[☃ image 1]]
Yes, $(1,3)$ is a solution of the graphed inequality.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes both scale by one. There is a dashed line representing an inequality that goes through the points zero, negative three and two, two. The shaded region is above the line.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Is $(-4,-5)$ a solution of the graphed inequality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$(-4,-5)$ is on the border of the shaded area.



[[☃ image 1]]

The graph shows a dashed line for the border, so the border is *not* included in the solution.
No, $(-4,-5)$ is *not* a solution of the graphed inequality.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes both scale by one. There is a dashed line representing an inequality that goes through the points negative there, negative three and zero, three. The shaded region is above the line.
